converter.title: Kitchen Unit Converter
converter.description: Convert grams, ounces, liters, tablespoons and other kitchen measurements instantly.

forWhom.title: Who is this for
forWhom.text: Especially useful when working with international recipes that use different measurement systems: metric (grams, liters) and imperial (ounces, pounds).

seoText.title: Kitchen Unit Converter
seoText.p1: Precision in measurement is a cornerstone of professional cooking. Different countries use different systems — metric and imperial — which can cause confusion when working with international recipes.
seoText.p2: European recipes typically use grams and milliliters, while American recipes rely on ounces, pounds, cups, and tablespoons. This converter bridges both worlds instantly.
seoText.p3: Convert: grams to ounces, kilograms to pounds, milliliters to liters, liters to fluid ounces, cups to milliliters, tablespoons to teaspoons — all in one place.
seoText.p4: The tool is especially useful for chefs working with international menus, culinary students, food bloggers, and anyone who wants to cook precisely.

items.q: How to convert grams to ounces?
items.a: 1 ounce = 28.35 grams. Divide grams by 28.35 to get ounces.

items.q: How many grams in a pound?
How many ml in a cup?
How many teaspoons in a tablespoon?

closingText.title: Unit calculator for cooks
closingText.text: This kitchen unit converter helps you quickly convert measurements used in different countries. European recipes use grams and milliliters, while American recipes use ounces, pounds, and cups. Use this tool to convert ingredients on the fly while cooking.

fishSeason.title: Fish Season Calendar

ingredientConverter.microtrust: Results calculated based on real ingredient density

popularConversions.title: Popular Kitchen Conversions
popularConversions.description: 1 cup of flour in grams, 1 tablespoon of butter in grams and other popular cooking conversions.
popularConversions.noData: No data available. Try again later.
popularConversions.seoP1: How many grams in a cup of flour? How much does a tablespoon of butter weigh? We answer these questions with pre-calculated conversions for popular kitchen ingredients.
popularConversions.seoP2: The table covers flour, sugar, butter, oil, honey and more. All values account for ingredient density.
popularConversions.seoP3: This page is automatically updated from our API — always current data.
popularConversions.closingText: Popular kitchen conversions is a collection of the most frequently searched cooking unit conversions. Ideal for chefs, pastry chefs, and food bloggers.

scale.title: Ingredient Scaler
scale.description: Scale ingredient amounts to a different number of portions — automatically.
scale.errorGeneral: Could not calculate. Please try again.
scale.seoP1: Have a recipe for 4 servings but cooking for 10? The ingredient scaler automatically recalculates amounts.
scale.seoP2: Enter the ingredient, amount, unit and portion count — the calculator adjusts the proportion and suggests an optimal unit.
scale.closingText: The ingredient scaler helps recalculate recipes for any number of servings. Perfect when cooking for a crowd or scaling down a recipe.

nutrition.title: Nutrition Calculator
nutrition.description: Calories, protein, fat and carbs for any ingredient — with localized names and photos.
nutrition.errorGeneral: Could not calculate. Please try again.
nutrition.seoP1: How many calories in 100g of flour? What is the macro breakdown of salmon? The nutrition calculator answers these questions — enter an ingredient and unit.
nutrition.seoP2: Data comes from our catalog database with real product photos and localized names in 4 languages.
nutrition.closingText: The nutrition calculator helps you quickly check calories, protein, fat and carbs for any cooking ingredient. An ideal tool for chefs and nutritionists.

suggestions.title: How many grams in a cup?
suggestions.description: Gram table — how much does a cup, tablespoon or teaspoon of various food products weigh.
suggestions.noData: No data available. Try again later.
suggestions.seoP1: How many grams in a cup of flour? How much does a tablespoon of sugar weigh? The table contains conversions for the most popular products.
suggestions.seoP2: All values account for ingredient density — data from our catalog with real photos.
suggestions.closingText: The gram table for kitchen ingredients helps quickly check ingredient weights in cups and spoons. Perfect when cooking from American recipes.

equivalents.title: Ingredient Equivalents
equivalents.description: Convert any ingredient to all measurement units — accounting for product density.
equivalents.errorGeneral: Could not convert. Please try again.
equivalents.seoP1: How many grams in a cup of flour? And how many ounces? The converter shows ingredient values in all units at once.
equivalents.seoP2: The tool uses product density from our catalog — results are more accurate than pure volume conversions.
equivalents.closingText: The ingredient equivalents converter shows how much a product weighs in grams, ounces, pounds, milliliters and cups — accounting for real density.

recipeAnalyzer.title: Recipe Analyzer
recipeAnalyzer.description: Add ingredients to get instant nutrition analysis, flavor radar chart, and smart suggestions to improve your recipe.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP1: The Recipe Analyzer combines three professional culinary tools in one interface: a nutrition calculator, a flavor profiler, and a recipe optimizer.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP2: Enter your ingredients with amounts in grams. The tool fetches real nutritional data from our catalog of 112+ ingredients and calculates total calories, protein, fat, carbohydrates, fiber, and sugar.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP3: The Flavor Radar Chart visualizes your recipe across 6 taste dimensions: sweetness, acidity, bitterness, umami, fat, and aroma. A balanced recipe scores higher.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP4: Based on the analysis, the Suggestion Engine recommends ingredients that fill flavor gaps, have high pairing scores, and add nutritional value.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP5: This tool is free and works with real data — no guessing, no AI hallucinations.
recipeAnalyzer.changeConcept: For further improvement, try changing ingredients manually

flavorPairing.title: Flavor Pairing Explorer
flavorPairing.description: Search any ingredient to discover its best flavor matches, culinary profile, and pairing scores.
flavorPairing.noPairings: No pairing data available for this ingredient yet.
flavorPairing.seoP1: Flavor pairing is a culinary concept based on the idea that ingredients sharing similar flavor compounds taste good together. Our pairing database combines three scoring dimensions:
flavorPairing.seoFlavor: Flavor Score — how well the ingredients complement each other across taste dimensions (sweetness, acidity, umami, bitterness, fat, aroma)
flavorPairing.seoNutrition: Nutrition Score — whether the combination creates a nutritionally balanced pairing
flavorPairing.seoCulinary: Culinary Score — based on traditional culinary traditions and professional cooking experience
flavorPairing.seoP2: The Pair Score is a weighted average of all three dimensions. Scores range from 0 to 10, where 10 is a perfect pairing.
flavorPairing.seoP3: Click on any pairing result to explore that ingredient further — navigate the entire flavor graph by following connections.
flavorPairing.seoP4: Our database contains 112+ ingredients with verified pairing data. This tool is free, fast, and based on real culinary data.

lab.title: Culinary Intelligence Lab
lab.description: Combine recipe analysis, nutrition, flavor profiling, and ingredient pairing in one powerful interface.
lab.subtitle: Analyze recipes, convert kitchen units, explore ingredient flavors and discover the best pairings.
lab.inputHint: e.g. 2 cups pasta, 3 tomatoes, 1 tbsp olive oil, basil
lab.noIngredients: Add some ingredients to begin analysis. Our system will calculate everything for you.
lab.clickIngredient: Click any ingredient to see its best pairings
lab.noPairings: No pairing data for this ingredient yet.
lab.seoTitle: What is the Culinary Intelligence Lab?
lab.seoP1: The Culinary Intelligence Lab combines five professional culinary tools in one interface: unit conversion, nutrition analysis, flavor profiling, recipe optimization, and ingredient pairing.
lab.seoP2: Enter your ingredients — the tool converts units, calculates nutrition, maps your flavor profile across 6 taste dimensions, and recommends ingredients to improve balance.
lab.seoP3: Click any ingredient to explore its pairing graph. Navigate from ingredient to ingredient discovering optimal combinations.
lab.seoP4: Our database contains 112+ ingredients with verified nutritional data and pairing scores. Free, fast, and based on real culinary science.

rules.lowAcidityDesc: The recipe lacks brightness. Adding an acid source will lift the flavor.
rules.fixAddAcid: Add an acid source like lemon, vinegar or tomato
rules.lowUmamiDesc: The recipe lacks depth and savory notes.
rules.fixAddUmami: Add umami-rich ingredients like cheese, soy sauce, or mushrooms
rules.lowFatDesc: The dish may feel thin. Adding a fat source will improve mouthfeel.
rules.fixAddFat: Add butter, olive oil, or cream
rules.lowAromaDesc: The recipe could use more aromatic complexity.
rules.fixAddAroma: Add garlic, basil, or fresh herbs
rules.lowSweetnessDesc: A touch of natural sweetness could balance the flavor.
rules.fixAddSweet: Add honey, onion, or carrot for natural sweetness
rules.lowBitternessDesc: A hint of bitterness adds complexity.
rules.fixAddBitter: Try black pepper, arugula, or dark greens
rules.highCarbsDesc: Over 65% of calories come from carbohydrates.
rules.fixReduceCarbs: Add protein-rich ingredients to balance macros
rules.lowProteinDesc: Under 12% of calories from protein — consider adding a protein source.
rules.fixAddProtein: Add chicken, egg, fish, or legumes
rules.highFatRatioDesc: Over 45% of calories come from fat.
rules.fixReduceFat: Reduce oil or butter, add lean ingredients
rules.fixAddFiber: Add broccoli, lentils, or whole grains
rules.highSugarDesc: Over 20% of calories come from sugar.
rules.dominanceDesc: One ingredient makes up over 60% of total weight.
rules.fixAddVariety: Add more ingredients for better balance
rules.missingFatDesc: The recipe has no oil, butter, or dairy for richness.
rules.fixAddFatSource: Add olive oil, butter, or cream
rules.missingAromaticsDesc: No herbs or spices detected. Aromatics add complexity.
rules.fixAddAromatics: Add garlic, basil, or black pepper
rules.missingProteinDesc: A grain-based recipe without meat, fish, egg, or legume.
rules.missingAcidDesc: Acidity is low and no obvious acid source found.

fishSeason.pairingBody: AI detected high umami levels in current catches. Use acid-rich pairings to lift the flavor profile.

seasonInsight.spring: Spring Insight: sea fish at peak — prioritize wild catches and cold-smoke techniques.
seasonInsight.summer: Summer Insight: freshwater varieties shine — go for grilling and citrus pairings.
seasonInsight.autumn: Autumn Insight: fatty fish reach peak umami — perfect for curing and confit.
seasonInsight.winter: Winter Insight: cold-water species at maximum quality — sushi and sashimi season.
